title: Open Catalyst 2020 Adsorbate-Surface DFT Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Official documentation for downloading and processing OC20.
Use for dataset construction, task definitions, and citation.
Open paper copy; useful for scale and challenge split details.

access.authentication: none_expected; full downloads are very large
instructions: Do not download the full OC20 dataset by default.
Start with metadata, official validation/test split manifests, or a small split/category subset.
Record system ID, adsorbate, surface, bulk composition, atomistic structure, energy/force target, split category, and relaxation step.

scale.download_size: very large; OC20 includes about 1.28M DFT relaxations and hundreds of millions of single-point evaluations

recommended_axes.name: adsorbate_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Chemical similarity over adsorbate formula, bonding, element composition, and molecular graph.

recommended_axes.name: surface_and_bulk_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity over surface composition, crystal structure, facet, and element set.

recommended_axes.name: oc20_split_category
recommended_axes.definition: Official ID/OOD Ads/OOD Cat/OOD Both challenge categories treated as operating points on a novelty curve.

recommended_axes.name: local_atomic_environment_support
recommended_axes.definition: Nearest-neighbor support over graph, coordination, SOAP-like, or learned atomistic representations.

useful_for: Auditing atomistic and materials foundation models under adsorbate/surface novelty.
Turning official OC20 OOD challenge splits into continuous SPECTRA curves.
Providing a major non-bio scientific ML benchmark where full-data use must be explicitly scoped.
leakage_risks: The same bulk, surface, adsorbate, or relaxation family can appear across splits.
Relaxation trajectory frames are highly correlated and should not be split naively.
Pretrained atomistic models may already include OC20 or close derivatives.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip surfaced OC20/OC22/OC25 as recurring catalyst generalization datasets; official Open Catalyst documentation confirms OC20 access and scale.
